heading: Linking
body:
Each resource includes one or more links to sub-resources and/or
related resources. Links are placed in the `links` field of an
entity, which is an array of link relation objects. Each link relation
has two fields:

| Field | Description                                                                                                                        |
| ----- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| rel   | Name (or type) of the relation. Many of these are considered Extension Relation Types and are prefixed by http\://mms.mongodb.com. |
| href  | The target URL (Uniform Resource Locator).                                                                                         |

All entities include at least one link relation called `self`, which
is simply its own URL (Uniform Resource Locator). When an entity is part of a list, then it only
includes the `self` link relation.

For more information, refer to the [Web Linking Specification](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8288). Note that although the
specification describes a format for including links in the HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol)
response headers, doing so is not a requirement. To make the
Atlas Administration API easily browsable, it includes the links in the
response body rather than in the response headers.
